name: DOF ASA
summary: DOF ASA, together with its subsidiaries, owns and operates a fleet of offshore vessels worldwide. The company operates through three segments: Platform Supply Vessel (PSV), Anchor Handling Tug Supply Vessel (AHTS), and Subsea Vessel and Subsea Engineering. It operates a fleet of PSVs that are used to transport oilfield products and supplies to offshore drilling and production facilities; AHTS used to set anchors for drilling rigs, and tow mobile drilling rigs and equipment from one location to another; and subsea vessels used for a range of subsea services. The company also provides subsea services comprising project management, engineering, construction and installation, life-of-field, decommissioning/field abandonment, and survey and positioning, as well as inspection, repair, and maintenance services. In addition, it offers marine management services, such as crewing, chartering, mobilization, maintenance, refurbishment, projects and new building, and compliance services. As of December 31, 2019, the company operated a fleet of 67 vessels, including 16 PSVs, 20 AHTSs, and 31 subsea/construction vessels, as well as 72 remote operated vehicles and 2 autonomous underwater vehicles. It operates in Brazil, the United Kingdom, Australia, Norway, Canada, the United States, Guyana, the Philippines, Angola, Argentina, and internationally. The company was founded in 1981 and is headquartered in Storeb√É¬∏, Norway.
